WALKERLAKE.EVENTS The CauseArtistsVisit

Walker Lake & Bighorn Sheep Resilience in the Desert

July 1–31, 2026

The Bighorn Crossing
847 Frontage Rd, Walker Lake, NV 89415

The Cause

This July, around twenty artists — painters, printmakers, ceramicists, metalworkers, some local, some from across the country — take over The Bighorn Crossing for a month of art about a lake that has given up more than 150 feet of itself within a lifetime, and the bighorn herd, Nevada’s largest, that still crosses the road nearby. Free, all month, indoors and across the outdoor grounds — in connection with Artown, in partnership with the Walker Basin Conservancy, working to return water to the lake.

Curated by Micky Kim · The Bighorn Crossing

Highlights

Patricia L. Cooke

Mixed-media fibers · Miami, FL

Hand-dyed, quilted color pushed toward beautiful excess. In cheeky, a herd of bighorn dance through rabbit brush while a scaled body — Cecil the Serpent? — slithers through the composition. Cooke is Senior Lecturer of Sculpture at the University of Miami.

Sigourney Gunther

Jewelry design · West Hollywood, CA

The Jeweled Bighorn — a ram built of beads and jewels, catching the desert light. Gunther is a jewelry designer and serves as an Arts Commissioner for the City of West Hollywood.

Ricardo Breceda

Outdoor metal sculpture · California

Monumental metal on the grounds year-round — bighorn, and a Paiute lake-serpent rising from the desert floor — from the sculptor behind Anza‑Borrego’s famous desert creatures.
